name: Add DELETE /tasks/{id} following the project error-envelope convention
prompt: Add a DELETE /tasks/{task_id} endpoint to this FastAPI app. Only admins may delete a task; deleting a task that does not exist must return a not-found error; a non-admin actor must be rejected. On success return the deleted task. Take an `actor_id` query parameter like the other endpoints. Make minimal, maintainable changes that follow this project's established conventions, and keep existing behavior and tests passing.

memory_seed.title: HTTP error envelope
memory_seed.summary: All HTTP error responses MUST use the JSON envelope {"error": {"code": <stable machine code string>, "message": <human-readable text>}} with the matching HTTP status. Implement client errors via the shared helper error_response(status, code, message) in app/errors.py (returns a fastapi.responses.JSONResponse). Do NOT raise bare HTTPException for client errors, because that produces FastAPI's default {"detail": ...} shape which clients do not parse.

memory_seed.title: Error codes
memory_seed.summary: Use the stable machine codes 'not_found' for missing resources and 'forbidden' for authorization failures in the error envelope's code field.

memory_seed.title: Roles
memory_seed.summary: Users have one of three roles: admin, member, viewer. Admin is the only role permitted to perform destructive operations.

warmup: We have decided that ALL HTTP error responses in this API must use a shared envelope {"error": {"code": <stable machine code string>, "message": <human-readable text>}}, instead of FastAPI's default {"detail": ...}. Implement a helper error_response(status, code, message) in app/errors.py that returns a fastapi.responses.JSONResponse, and refactor the existing client errors in app/main.py (the 404 and 403 in patch_task, the 422 in create_task) to use it. Use lowercase snake_case codes like 'not_found' and 'forbidden'. Briefly state the convention you established.

requirements.description: Admin can delete an existing task and gets the task back
Error body uses the project envelope {error:{code,message}} (memory-only knowledge)
